Centralized image pipeline present: compute galleries (37) and 16 virtual machine image templates that include security-related features (e.g., SecurityType) indicate the tenant already builds and distributes curated images, so plan to produce hardened OS images via Shared Image Gallery / image templates (Image Builder) and automate their distribution to the regions/zones where the 3-tier VMs will run.

Managed identity usage is ubiquitous on compute: ~98.8% of virtual machines report SystemAssigned and/or UserAssigned identities and image templates use UserAssigned identities, so plan to assign user‑assigned managed identities to the new VMs/VMSS to access Key Vault and backup services rather than embedding secrets.

Backup infrastructure and encryption are in place: 90 Recovery Services vaults (infrastructure encryption Enabled) and 18 dataprotection backup vaults exist across the tenant, so plan to register the tier VMs with a Recovery Services vault and apply a daily backup policy with infrastructure encryption and appropriate retention/soft‑delete settings.

Horizontal scaling is an established pattern: VM scale sets (29) often appear with autoscale settings, Standard load balancers and related networking in frequent itemsets, so implement the business‑logic tier as a VMSS (or containerized autoscaling pool) fronted by a Standard Load Balancer with health probes and autoscale rules to achieve application‑level redundancy.

Networked secret protection is standard: 169 Key Vaults have enablePurgeProtection True and long soft‑delete retention (90 days) with network ACLs set (default action Allow 63% / Deny 37%), so plan to store image and backup credentials in Key Vault with purge protection and restrict vault access via private endpoints or network rules consistent with tenant practice.

Zone redundancy is not commonly enabled by default: managed environments are zoneredundant True only ~10.5% and serverfarms zoneredundant True only ~1.7%, so if you need multi‑zone availability explicitly choose zone‑redundant SKUs or deploy VMSS instances across availability zones rather than relying on a default zonal configuration.

Standard network SKUs are the norm: Load Balancers are Standard (100%) and Public IPs are Standard (~95.9%), so use Standard SKU Public IPs and Standard Load Balancer for internal/external traffic to align with tenant resiliency and operational expectations.

Telemetry and alerting are widely deployed: 549 Application Insights components, 149 Log Analytics workspaces and many metricalerts exist, so plan to onboard new VMs/VMSS to Log Analytics/App Insights and create metric alerts and autoscale triggers to integrate the 3‑tier application into existing monitoring practices.
